video:video.video:title: Britney Spears enters substance abuse treatment facility
video:video.video:description: Britney Spears has checked herself into substance abuse treatment facility just over a month after she was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV pushes back against President Donald Trump’s comments in feud over Iran war
video:video.video:description: U.S.-born Pope Leo XIV pushed back Monday on President Donald Trump’s broadside against him over the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Tyson Fury's family knew he'd return to the ring
video:video.video:description: Boxer Tyson Fury's family knew he would return to the ring, even if the Gypsy King himself didn't. He beat Arslanbek Makhmudov on Saturday, and wants to fight Anthony Joshua next.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo hits back at Trump over Iran war
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV pushed back against President Donald Trump's criticism of his stance on the Iran war. He said that the Vatican’s appeals for peace and reconciliation are rooted in the Gospel, and that he didn’t fear the Trump administration.

video:video.video:title: Delta Goodrem is now a Eurovision super fan
video:video.video:description: Delta Goodrem, who is representing Australia at the 2026 Eurovision Song Contest, is ready to host watch parties in future years.

video:video.video:title: Ocean protections clash with mining pressure in Indonesia's most diverse marine ecosystem
video:video.video:description: Raja Ampat, a remote archipelago in eastern Indonesia, has been long regarded as a model for ocean conservation.

video:video.video:title: Trump lambasts Pope Leo XIV, extending feud over Iran war with first American pontiff
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump delivered an extraordinary broadside against Pope Leo XIV on Sunday night, saying he didn't think the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US military blockade of Iran's ports to begin Monday morning
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and the U.S. military say a U.S. blockade of all Iranian ports and coastal areas will begin on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Pope departs for Africa as Trump issues scathing Truth Social post
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV is embarking Monday on the first-ever papal trip to Algeria, aiming to promote Christian-Muslim coexistence at a time of global conflict and honor the locally born inspiration of his religious spirituality, St.

video:video.video:title: Hungarian analyst on 'historical win' for Magyar's Tisza party
video:video.video:description: Hungarian political analyst Bulcsú Hunyadi described Péter Magyar's election victory as "historical," but warned there were "obstacles in the system" facing his Tisza party.

video:video.video:title: Israeli strike near the entrance to Red Cross offices in Lebanon's Tyre
video:video.video:description: An Israeli strike near the entrance to Red Cross offices in the coastal city of Tyre in southern Lebanon killed a wounded person who was being transported to the centre, damaging several Red Cross vehicles and shattering the centre's windows on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Hosting solar could have been a financial lifeline for this Ohio farmer
video:video.video:description: Wayne Greier hoped to sign a deal with a utility to host solar on his acreage for some $540,000 in annual lease payments. But his community blocked the plan.

video:video.video:title: Peru's presidential candidates criticize polling station issues in Lima affecting thousands of voters
video:video.video:description: Peruvian voters will have to wait until at least Monday to learn the outcome of Sunday’s presidential election after the process was mired with logistical issues that left thousands of people in the country and abroad unable to cast ballots.

video:video.video:title: Oscar Isaac and Carey Mulligan share how they consume art
video:video.video:description: Oscar Isaac and Carey Mulligan share the parts about consuming art that excite them the most. Season two of their Netflix series "Beef" premieres on April 16.

video:video.video:title: Paris Fury's parenting advice for Molly Mae and Tommy Fury
video:video.video:description: Boxer Tyson Fury's wife Paris passes on family advice to brother-in-law Tommy Fury and his partner, reality TV star Molly-Mae Hague.

video:video.video:title: Rain across northern Michigan heightens concerns of river flooding, dam overflows
video:video.video:description: Gov. Gretchen Whitmer has declared a state of emergency at the Cheboygan Lock and Dam Complex as record snowfall in March and the recent rain have elevated water levels.

video:video.video:title: Peru election stretches into a second day after ballot delivery failures
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Peruvians were back at the polls on Monday for a second day of voting after failure to deliver ballots to voting centers extended Sunday’s election by a day.

video:video.video:title: Starmer says UK will "continue to stay out" of Iran war due to national interest
video:video.video:description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer described the two-week ceasefire as "highly fragile" and stressed that Lebanon should be included in it.

video:video.video:title: Pope says he has no fear of Trump following US president's scathing post
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV said he had “no fear of the Trump administration" when he responded to a reporter's question on board the papal plane en route to Algiers on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Historical documents reach California on rare tour celebrating the nation's 250th anniversary
video:video.video:description: Some of the United States’ most important historical documents have arrived in California as part of the country’s 250th anniversary commemoration.

video:video.video:title: Trump: Strait blockade is in effect but Iran wants a deal 'very badly'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump confirmed Monday a U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports is underway, saying “we can’t let a country blackmail” the world.

video:video.video:title: Lee Sung-Jin on why he wanted to make 'Beef' an anthology series
video:video.video:description: Lee Sung-Jin shares why he wanted to expand "Beef" into an anthology series. Season two starts streaming on Netflix on April 16.

video:video.video:title: Election loss for Hungarian Prime Minister Orbán has ripple effects for Trump, US conservatives
video:video.video:description: In a stunning blow to Viktor Orban, Hungarian voters have ousted the prime minister after 16 years in power, which could have significant reverberations in the United States.

video:video.video:title: Colombia approves plan to cull roaming hippos linked to Pablo Escobar
video:video.video:description: Colombian officials on Monday authorized a plan to cull dozens of hippos roaming freely through a region in the center of the country, where they threaten villagers and displace native species years after notorious drug lord Pablo Escobar brought in the first ones.

video:video.video:title: Hungarian Tisza politician performs stage dance to celebrate election victory
video:video.video:description: Hungarian politician Zsolt Hegedűs from the winning Tisza party performed a viral stage dance while celebrating election victory in Budapest on Sunday night.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo's Chicago alma mater reacts to his anti-war comments and President Trump's response
video:video.video:description: The academic dean of the school where the pope studied supports the Catholic leader's anti-war comments on Easter, saying they align with gospel teachings.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo calls for peace at start of his Africa tour
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV called for peace and the end of “neocolonial tendencies” in world affairs on Monday during the first papal visit to Algeria, all while facing an extraordinary broadside by President Donald Trump over his criticism of the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Dave Chappelle stands firm through backlash and considers revisiting ‘Chappelle’s Show’
video:video.video:description: Dave Chappelle finds clarity away from the spotlight in the Ohio village where he lives. In an interview, he reflects on his comedic voice before the opening of a restored 19th-century schoolhouse that now houses a public radio station.

video:video.video:title: Michaela Coel loved being part of an all-female cast
video:video.video:description: Michaela Coel stars as a former costume designer opposite Anne Hathaway, FKA Twigs, Hunter Schafer and Kaia Gerber in "Mother Mary." (April 13)

video:video.video:title: Thailand kicks off Songkran festival celebrating new year with signature water fight
video:video.video:description: An annual water festival kicked off in Bangkok on Monday, marking the start of the Thai new year.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV pays visit to the great Mosque of Algiers
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited the Great Mosque in Algiers, Algeria, on Monday in the first day of an Africa trip that will also take him to Cameroon, Angola, and Equatorial Guinea.

video:video.video:title: Afghanistan's capital is in the grip of a water crisis
video:video.video:description: Nestled in a high-altitude valley in Afghanistan's Hindu Kush mountain range, Kabul is rapidly running out of water.

video:video.video:title: Officials warn of crisis in Gaza City as residents struggle with rodent 'infestations'
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in Gaza City are reporting massive infestations of rodents, as life in the displacement tents has become increasingly brutal.

video:video.video:title: Prince Harry and Meghan visit children's hospital during low-key, privately funded Australia visit
video:video.video:description: Prince Harry and his wife Meghan, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex, arrived in Melbourne on Tuesday for their first Australian visit since their official royal tour in 2018.

video:video.video:title: President Trump and first lady host King and Queen of the Netherlands, PM Jetten at White House
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump welcomed King Willem-Alexander and Queen Máxima of the Netherlands to the White House on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Spanish Prime Minister Sanchez meets with Chinese President Xi during Beijing visit
video:video.video:description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez met with Chinese president Xi Jinping on Tuesday, during his visit to China as Spain seeks to strengthen its political and commercial ties with Beijing.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 13
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, April 13th: EU and UN Security Council discuss Strait of Hormuz and the possibility of a U.S. blockade; Second day of Peruvian voting; Pope visits Great Mosque of Algiers; Philippine President Marcos seeks to quell health concerns

video:video.video:title: Drivers across the US are feeling the pinch as both new car and gas prices continue to climb
video:video.video:description: American motorists are feeling pain at the pump as well as the dealership (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Israel and Hezbollah clash in strategic Lebanese village ahead of official talks
video:video.video:description: Israel has stepped up its strikes and ground invasion in southern Lebanon, where it intends to create a security zone along the Litani River, almost 20 miles (32 kilometers) from the border.

video:video.video:title: Ethiopian Orthodox Christians in Washington celebrate Easter
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of Ethiopian Orthodox Christians packed tightly into Washington, D.C.’s DSK Mariam Church to stand vigil as their Easter Sunday unfolded.

video:video.video:title: Hungarian election winner Péter Magyar says his government ‘will be a constructive partner' in EU
video:video.video:description: Hungarian election winner Péter Magyar promised a beginning of a new era during an international news conference after his Tisza party's victory in Budapest on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Bot-sketball star may take the court by storm in Japan
video:video.video:description: Toyota Motor Corp. unveiled its latest AI-powered basketball robot, CUE7, on Sunday, giving the media a preview during a rehearsal at Toyota Arena Tokyo ahead of its halftime debut at a B.League game.

video:video.video:title: Peru election stretches into a second day after ballot delivery failures
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Peruvians were back at the polls on Monday for a second day of voting after failure to deliver ballots to voting centers extended Sunday’s election by a day.

video:video.video:title: Humanoid robots show off boxing and language skills in Hong Kong
video:video.video:description: What will the next generation of robots be able to do? In Hong Kong, the machines have been showing off their ability to teach, dance and even fight.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he posted an image of himself as a 'doctor' not Jesus and won’t apologize to Pope Leo
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump admits to posting an image of himself as a healer, seeming to make comparisons between himself and Jesus Christ.

video:video.video:title: Rep. Eric Swalwell of California says he will resign from Congress after sexual assault allegations
video:video.video:description: Democratic Rep. Eric Swalwell says he will resign from Congress as a number of sexual assault allegations have come to light.

video:video.video:title: Faithful express excitement as Pope Leo XIV celebrates Mass in Algeria during visit
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV held a special Mass inside the Basilica of Our Lady of Africa in the Algerian capital on Monday as he began the first leg of his African tour.

video:video.video:title: Anne Hathaway on 'Mother Mary' vs 'Devil Wears Prada'
video:video.video:description: Anne Hathaway contrasts her "Mother Mary" spiral with "Devil Wears Prada" character Andy Sachs’ optimism, saying they share nothing in common beyond her. (April 13)

video:video.video:title: Happy 69th birthday to Fatou, the world's oldest gorilla living in captivity
video:video.video:description: The world’s oldest gorilla living in captivity has celebrated her 69th birthday. Fatou spent Monday munching on cherry tomatoes, beets, leeks and lettuce at the Berlin Zoo (AP video shot by Fanny Brodersen).

video:video.video:title: Chinese President Xi meets with visiting crown prince of Abu Dhabi
video:video.video:description: The crown prince of Abu Dhabi was in Beijing on Tuesday to meet with China's leader Xi Jinping. Sheikh Khaled bin Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan met with Xi as part of an official visit to China.
video:video.video:tag: Khaled bin Mohammed bin Zayed Al Nahyan

video:video.video:title: Philippine President Marcos debunks health rumors with jumping jacks
video:video.video:description: An annoyed Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. did a few rounds of jumping jack exercise and jogged briefly outside his office in front of journalists Monday to disprove rumors of his failing health.

video:video.video:title: Volunteers clean up animals after oil spill in the Black Sea
video:video.video:description: An oil spill was detected in the Black Sea, 11 kilometres (6.8 miles) offshore from Anapa in Russia’s Krasnodar region, local authorities said Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Anne Hathaway: 'Mother Mary' vs 'Devil Wears Prada'
video:video.video:description: In new psychological thriller "Mother Mary," Anne Hathaway plays the polar opposite of her "The Devil Wears Prada" character, Andy Sachs.

video:video.video:title: Hezbollah official says group won't abide by agreements from Lebanon-Israel talks
video:video.video:description: A senior Hezbollah political official told The Associated Press on Monday - a day before Israeli and Lebanese officials are set to hold the opening meeting in the two countries’ first direct talks in decades - that the militant group will not abide by any agreement made in those negotiations.

video:video.video:title: Ian McKellen and Michaela Coel are 'silly' about each other
video:video.video:description: In Steven Soderbergh’s “The Christophers,” stars Ian McKellen and Michaela Coel make one of the more memorably on-screen pairs in recent memory.

video:video.video:title: Ian McKellen's 'greatest delight'
video:video.video:description: Ian McKellen - who stars in new dark comedy "The Christophers" - looks back on his career.

video:video.video:title: Boat strike in the eastern Pacific Ocean leaves 2 dead, US military says
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said it carried out another strike Monday on a boat accused of trafficking drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing two people.

video:video.video:title: 'Absolutely absurd': Catholics visiting the Vatican react to Trump's pope comments
video:video.video:description: Catholics in Vatican City, locals and tourists alike, reacted Tuesday to an ongoing dispute between U.S.

video:video.video:title: Major storm hitting remote US islands, Saipan mayor warns of danger
video:video.video:description: Super Typhoon Sinlaku is approaching Saipan in the Northern Mariana Islands, where the mayor says objects are flying “left and right.”

video:video.video:title: Lebanese and Israelis anticipate outcome of upcoming Lebanon-Israel meeting in Washington
video:video.video:description: Many residents in Lebanon and Israel on Tuesday expressed pessimism that the upcoming Lebanon-Israel meeting in Washington would not be fruitful.

video:video.video:title: Officials warn of crisis in Gaza City as residents struggle with rodent 'infestations'
video:video.video:description: Displaced Palestinians in Gaza are reporting infestations of rodents, as life in the tents has become increasingly unbearable

video:video.video:title: How Homelander zeroed in on the memes
video:video.video:description: Viral memes of “The Boys” super villain Homelander inspired a pitch for the show’s final season, by the actor who plays him, Antony Starr.

video:video.video:title: WNBA draft prospects describe their fit in three words
video:video.video:description: On the WNBA Draft orange carpet, draft prospects described their look in three words.

video:video.video:title: Ian McKellen's 'greatest delight'
video:video.video:description: Sir Ian McKellen - who stars in Steven Soderbergh's new movie "'The Christophers" as an artist nearing the end of his life - looks back on his career, saying it's a privilege that his work has touched people far and wide.

video:video.video:title: Monster typhoon in the Pacific Ocean is bearing down on group of remote US islands
video:video.video:description: A super typhoon is taking aim at several remote U.S. islands in the Pacific Ocean. Super Typhoon Sinlaku is on track to barrel over the Northern Mariana Islands late Tuesday local time with widespread rain and flooding along with destructive winds that could cause lengthy power outages, the National

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 14
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, April 14th: Officials say Pakistan proposed new round of U.S.-Iran talks; Lebanon and Israel to hold direct negotiations; Congressman Eric Swalwell to resign; Massive storm threatens remote U.S.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he won’t apologize to Pope Leo
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said he would not apologize for sharply criticizing Pope Leo saying that the pontiff “went public” in his criticisms of the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: UN chief says international law is ‘being trampled’ especially in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: U.N. Secretary-General Antonio Guterres is urging a resumption of U.S.-Iran negotiations, a continuation of the ceasefire and the respect for freedom of navigation, including in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Residents recall attack on a Tehran residential complex during war
video:video.video:description: During a government-organized media tour, Associated Press journalists observed the aftermath of a devastating airstrike that struck the Borujerdi residential complex in southeastern Tehran on on March 4.

video:video.video:title: Israeli ambassador says Israel and Lebanon are ‘on the same side of the equation’ after DC talks
video:video.video:description: Israeli Ambassador to the U.S. Yechiel Leiter said Tuesday the first negotiation talks between Israel and Lebanon were "positive", saying about the two countries "we are on the same side of the equation."

video:video.video:title: 'Absolutely absurd' - visitors at Vatican City react to Trump's Pope comments
video:video.video:description: Visitors Vatican City, locals and tourists alike, reacted Tuesday to an ongoing dispute between U.S.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 14
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, April 14th: Israeli and Lebanese delegations meet in Washington; IMF unveils global growth and inflation reports; Prince Harry and wife Meghan visit Australia; Rapper Offset returns to stage days after shooting.

video:video.video:title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku pounds remote US islands in the Pacific with ferocious winds
video:video.video:description: A super typhoon with ferocious winds and heavy rains is battering a group of remote U.S. islands in the Pacific Ocean.

video:video.video:title: Powerball jackpot is expanding to include players in the UK
video:video.video:description: The lottery game that has made millionaires in the United States will expand this summer to include players in England, Scotland and other parts of the United Kingdom.

video:video.video:title: Israeli police find about 70 Palestinian men hiding in garbage truck attempting to cross into Israel
video:video.video:description: Israeli police say they found about 70 Palestinian men hiding in a garbage truck as they attempted to cross into Israel, apparently in search of work.

video:video.video:title: People from multiple faiths share their thoughts on Trump's words about the pope
video:video.video:description: People from multiple faiths share their thoughts on Trump's words about the pope

video:video.video:title: The US blockade on Iran casts further doubt on the fragile ceasefire
video:video.video:description: The standoff between the United States and Iran deepened Tuesday as the U.S. declared it had blockaded Iran’s ports.

video:video.video:title: Ben & Jerry's co-founder wants the company to be independent once more. Here's the scoop.
video:video.video:description: As Ben & Jerry’s celebrated its annual Free Cone Day, co-founder Ben Cohen was focused on a different sort of freedom, calling on Ben & Jerry’s owner The Magnum Ice Cream Co. to sell the brand.

video:video.video:title: New trial over Diego Maradona's death resumes in Argentina against 7 health care professionals
video:video.video:description: Almost a year after the trial for the death of football idol Diego Maradona collapsed amid a scandal, a court on the outskirts of Buenos Aires began a new process on Tuesday to clarify the circumstances surrounding the passing that moved Argentina in 2020.

video:video.video:title: What it's really like covering the WNBA draft?
video:video.video:description: What does it actually take to cover the WNBA draft? Here’s a behind-the-scenes look from the orange carpet.

video:video.video:title: The Trump and Leo chronicles: A president and a pope square off over Iran and its aftermath
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV and President Donald Trump have long been on a rhetorical collision course. Now, their disagreement over the Iran war has escalated in unusual fashion, and their comments show how differently each see the conflict and its impact.

video:video.video:title: Attorney for suspect in attack at Sam Altman’s home says he was in midst of 'mental health crisis'
video:video.video:description: San Francisco District Attorney disputed that he was overcharged, saying Daniel Moreno-Gama carried out a “targeted attack on Mr.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV in Algeria walks in footsteps of his spiritual father, St. Augustine
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV walked Tuesday in the footsteps of his spiritual father, St. Augustine, making a pilgrimage to the archaeological ruins in Algeria where the fifth-century titan of early Christianity lived, died and wrote some of the most important works in Western thought.

video:video.video:title: A 5.7 earthquake jolts rural Nevada near Carson City, causing some damage
video:video.video:description: The temblor hit just before 6:30 p.m. local time, the U.S. Geological Survey said. It was centered about 13 miles east of the town of Silver Springs.

video:video.video:title: Hathaway and Coel's 'instant bond'
video:video.video:description: Anne Hathaway explains how the collaborations on upcoming thriller "Mother Mary" stopped her feeling helpless in the role of an unravelling pop star, while her co-star Michaela Cole discusses their instant bond.

video:video.video:title: How a US blockade near the Strait of Hormuz could work
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump's blockade is spurring questions about international law and leaves doubts about whether the pressure tactic will force Tehran to reopen the vital Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: We asked WNBA draft prospects what they’re obsessed with right now
video:video.video:description: What are WNBA draft prospects obsessed with right now? From shows to routines, here’s what they told us.

video:video.video:title: California woman says she was raped by Rep. Eric Swalwell in 2018
video:video.video:description: Lonna Drews said she believes Swalwell drugged her and then sexually assaulted her at a hotel. The AP does not typically name people who say they've been sexually assaulted unless they come forward publicly.

video:video.video:title: Severe storms leave damage behind in Kansas town
video:video.video:description: A day after severe storms damaged communities in the Plains and the Midwest, forecasters warned that storms could bring giant hail, tornadoes and severe wind gusts to the regions again on Tuesday afternoon and evening.

video:video.video:title: Harvey Weinstein is going on trial again in a New York rape case
video:video.video:description: Jury selection begins in New York in the rape trial of onetime movie mogul, Harvey Weinstein (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: Ex-Michigan coach Sherrone Moore gets probation for confrontation with woman who disclosed affair
video:video.video:description: Former Michigan football coach Sherrone Moore was placed on probation over a confrontation with his executive assistant soon after he was fired for having an inappropriate relationship with her (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: New video of divers recovering Artemis II astronauts after splashdown
video:video.video:description: New video when U.S. Navy divers reach and open the Orion capsule of Artemis II reaching the astronauts for the first time in 10 days after their trip to the moon.

video:video.video:title: Mexico's Claudia Sheinbaum takes a firmer stance with Trump on migrant deaths and Cuba
video:video.video:description: Mexico’s President Claudia Sheinbaum is taking a firmer stance against U.S. President Donald Trump, condemning migrant deaths in ICE detention and opposing policies toward Cuba.

video:video.video:title: Ducklings reunited with their mother after officers rescue them from a storm drain
video:video.video:description: Body camera footage shows law enforcement in Redmond, Washington, rescuing ducklings from a storm drain and reuniting the flock with its likely mother.

video:video.video:title: Ducklings reunite with mother after being rescued from storm drain
video:video.video:description: Body camera footage shows law enforcement in Redmond, Washington, rescuing ducklings from a storm drain and reuniting the flock with its likely mother.

video:video.video:title: GOP Rep. Clay Fuller sworn-in on Capitol Hill after special election win
video:video.video:description: Clay Fuller now represents the congressional district of former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene.

video:video.video:title: Martha Kelly talks working with Zendaya on 'Euphoria'
video:video.video:description: Martha Kelly details her first day working on the “Euphoria” set with Zendaya. Kelly plays Laurie, a drug lord who terrorizes Zendaya’s character in the hit HBO series.

video:video.video:title: Young voters in Hungary played crucial role in ending Orbán's 16-year rule
video:video.video:description: On Sunday, young voters celebrated the victory of pro-European candidate Péter Magyar. Polls showed 65% of voters under 30 supported Magyar before the vote.

video:video.video:title: Dave Chappelle is 'considering' revisiting ‘Chappelle’s Show’
video:video.video:description: Dave Chappelle says his thoughts about revisiting “Chappelle’s Show” have changed recently.

video:video.video:title: Bluebells magically take over Belgian forest
video:video.video:description: Bluebell flowers were in full bloom on Tuesday in the Belgian city of Halle. Tourists and photographers visited Hallerbos forest to experience the annual bloom.

video:video.video:title: Italian premier Meloni pushes back on 'unacceptable' Trump comments towards Pope Leo
video:video.video:description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Tuesday described U.S. President Donald Trump's comments towards Pope Leo XIV as "unacceptable".

video:video.video:title: Surveillance camera video shows moment of Turkey school shooting in which at least 16 wounded
video:video.video:description: A former student opened fire at a high school in southeastern Turkey on Tuesday, wounding at least 16 people, before killing himself, an official said.

video:video.video:title: Rising meat prices threaten a Minnesota gambling tradition
video:video.video:description: Meat raffles have been a fixture in bars and American Legion posts in the state for decades, but rising costs and fixed prize limits have put the squeeze on charitable organizations which rely on the fundraisers.

video:video.video:title: Families scour Mexican cartel land for missing. UN sees institutional shortcomings on state searches
video:video.video:description: Families of the missing in Mexico have been left to carry out their own searches, digging at suspected clandestine grave sites across cartel-controlled areas.

video:video.video:title: Children killed in Lebanon as Israeli strikes hit homes far from front lines of war with Hezbollah
video:video.video:description: Of more than 2,000 people killed in Lebanon since Israel's current war with Iran-backed Hezbollah began, 168 are children.

video:video.video:title: Canada's prime minister suspends fuel tax in first move after securing majority
video:video.video:description: Mark Carney has suspended the federal fuel excise tax in response to the Iran war. The announcement is his first act after securing a majority government.

video:video.video:title: Gunman opens fire at high school in Turkey, wounding at least 16 before killing himself
video:video.video:description: A former student opened fire at a high school in southeast Turkey on Tuesday, wounding at least 16 people before killing himself, an official said.

video:video.video:title: Bangladeshis celebrate beginning of Bengali New Year with colourful parade, music and dance
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people in Bangladesh on Tuesday ushered in the Bengali New Year 1433 with colorful processions and cultural festivities.

video:video.video:title: Washington Capitals captain Alex Ovechkin discusses his future plans
video:video.video:description: Washington Capitals captain and NHL all-time goals leader Alexander Ovechkin discusses his future plans as he considers retirement after 21 seasons.

video:video.video:title: Israeli settlers in West Bank block road Palestinian children use to walk to school
video:video.video:description: In the rolling hills of the occupied West Bank, a group of children sat and waited on Monday alongside the newly erected fence as their parents and leaders in their village, Umm al-Khair, demanded their passage.

video:video.video:title: House approves aviation safety bill based on deadly midair collision near Washington
video:video.video:description: An aviation safety bill seeking to address lessons learned from last year’s midair collision of a jet with an Army helicopter near Washington, DCl was approved by the House Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Powerful winds from Super Typhoon Sinlaku continue to impact US islands in the Pacific
video:video.video:description: A super typhoon steadily battered a pair of remote U.S. islands in the Pacific Ocean with ferocious winds and relentless rains.

video:video.video:title: WATCH: Art enthusiast left stunned after winning a $1M Picasso with a $117 raffle ticket
video:video.video:description: A Parisian art enthusiast has won a Pablo Picasso painting with a $117 raffle ticket. Ari Hodara was thrilled to learn he won following Tuesday's draw at Christie’s auction house in Paris.

video:video.video:title: Major Colorado pileup involving about 70 vehicles sends 8 to the hospital
video:video.video:description: Officials say crashes involving about 70 vehicles have bottlenecked a section of a snowy Colorado highway and sent eight people to the hospital.

video:video.video:title: Another US strike on suspected drug boat in the eastern Pacific kills 4
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military launched a strike on another boat accused of carrying drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean on Tuesday, killing four people in the fourth such attack announced in the past few days.
